## figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map, list_code_components, get_code_component_info)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (load first when available):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_figma flows
- /figma-generate-diagram — MANDATORY before calling generate_diagram

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branc… [truncated]

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- agentation: Add Agentation visual feedback toolbar to a Next.js project
- agentation-self-driving: Autonomous design critique mode using the Agentation annotation toolbar. Use when the user asks to "critique this page," "add design annotations," "review the UI," "self-driving mode," "auto-annotate," or wants an AI agent to autonomously add design feedback annotations to a web page via the browser. Requires the Agentation toolbar to be installed on the target page and agent-browser skill to be available.
- taste: Master design skill. Evaluates, guides, and refines UI/web design to match Stefan's editorial-minimal aesthetic. Use for any design task — it replaces remembering 18 separate design skills. Gets better over time.
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

content.text: Now the plan:

input.prompt:
Context: Stefan is a product designer building a prototype for Widforss (Swedish/Norwegian outdoor e-commerce brand). Their production site runs Next.js + Sanity CMS + Centra (e-commerce platform). 

He's built a TikTok/Reels-style vertical video feed prototype (static HTML with 5 Instagram reel clips). The goal is to strengthen social proof and community engagement on their e-commerce site — Instagram reach is too low, so they want an owned solution.

The prototype currently has:
- Vertical video feed with auto-snap scrolling
- Thumbnails sidebar, progress bar, mute toggle
- Left panel with title, timestamp, "Shop the look" CTA
- Infinite scroll with reshuffled batches

I need a comprehensive plan for how this should work in production with their stack. Think about:

1. **Sanity schema design** — how to model Shorts content (video, metadata, linked products, creator info, tags)
2. **Video hosting/delivery** — where to store/serve vertical videos (Sanity assets? Mux? Cloudinary? CDN considerations for autoplay performance)
3. **Centra product linking** — how "Shop the look" connects to their product catalog, deep linking to PDPs
4. **User-generated content (UGC)** — how could customers/community upload their own clips? Upload flow, moderation pipeline, Sanity as moderation dashboard
5. **Next.js implementation** — SSR vs client, lazy loading, preloading strategy, mobile responsiveness
6. **Cool features they might not have thought of** — product tagging on video timeline, creator profiles, engagement metrics, hashtag/collection feeds, shoppable overlays, affiliate tracking for UGC creators, AI auto-tagging
7. **Mobile experience** — this is desktop-first but mobile is where short video lives. Touch gestures, fullscreen, PWA considerations

Be specific about Sanity schema shapes, API patterns, and architectural decisions. This is going to their IT department as a proposal alongside the prototype. Write it as a clear, structured plan they can evaluate.

Output as a well-structured markdown document. Be opinionated — recommend specific services where appropriate. Think Norwegian market (Vipps, Klarna already in use).

## 1. Sanity Schema Design

### 1.1 Core Document: `short`

```js

    {

          { name: 'centraDisplayUri', type: 'string', title: 'Centra Display URI (slug)' },
```

### 1.2 Supporting Document: `creator`

```js

    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'handle', type: 'slug', description: '@handle for display' },
    { name: 'avatar', type: 'image', options: { hotspot: true } },

```

### 1.3 Supporting Documents: `shortsTag` and `shortsCollection`

```js
{
  name: 'shortsTag',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },  // e.g. "vinterturer", "layering"
    { name: 'slug', type: 'slug', options: { source: 'name' } },
  ],
}

// shortsCollection — curated group, e.g. "Vinterjakten 2026"
{
  name: 'shortsCollection',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'title', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'slug', type: 'slug', options: { source: 'title' } },

```

### 1.4 Singleton Document: `shortsFeedSettings`

```js

  name: 'shortsFeedSettings',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'pinnedShorts', type: 'array', of: [{ type: 'reference', to: [{ type: 'short' }] }], description: 'Always shown first in the feed, in this order' },
    { name: 'defaultDepartment', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'feedAlgorithm', type: 'string', options: { list: ['chronological', 'priority', 'engagement'] }, initialValue: 'priority' },
  ],
}
```

---

## 2. Video Hosting and Delivery

**Recommendation: Mux.**

Rationale:
- Mux has a first-party Sanity plugin (`sanity-plugin-mux-input`) making uploads trivial from the studio.
- Adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS) out of the box -- critical for vertical video where users are on 4G/5G.
- Automatic thumbnail generation at any timestamp (no manual screenshots).
- Mux Data gives you engagement analytics per-video (completion rate, rebuffer %, average watch time) with no extra integration.
- Storyboard sprites for seek preview come free.
- Mux is already used by other Scandinavian e-commerce companies on Sanity stacks.

**Why not Cloudinary or Sanity native assets:**
- Sanity's native file hosting has no transcoding, no adaptive bitrate, no analytics. Unusable for a video-first feature.
- Cloudinary's video is adequate but its Sanity integration is weaker and you do not get engagement analytics without extra tooling.

### Delivery Architecture

```
Sanity Studio (upload) → Mux (transcode + store)
                                    ↓
                        Mux Streaming URL (HLS)
                                    ↓
              Next.js <video> / hls.js / Mux Player React
```

**Mux Player React** (`@mux/mux-player-react`) is the recommended player component. It handles:
- HLS adaptive streaming
- Poster/thumbnail from playback ID
- Preload strategies (`metadata` vs `auto`)
- Mux Data tracking automatically

### Cost Estimate

Mux pricing (as of 2025): ~$0.007/min encoded + $0.003/min delivered. For a store with 100 shorts averaging 30 seconds each, initial encoding cost is about $3.50. Delivery at 50,000 views/month (25,000 minutes) would be roughly $75/month. Well within reason for an e-commerce site.

---

## 3. Centra Product Linking

### 3.1 Architecture

Centra is the source of truth for products, prices, and stock. Sanity stores only the reference (product ID + display URI), never the product data itself.

```
Sanity (short.products[].centraProductId)
            ↓ at render time
Next.js API route or server component
            ↓
Centra CheckoutAPI / ShopAPI
            ↓
Live price, stock, images, variant data
```

### 3.2 "Shop the Look" Drawer

When the user clicks "Shop the look", a slide-up drawer appears over the video (not a page navigation). This drawer:

1. Calls a Next.js API route: `GET /api/shorts/products?ids=123,456,789`
2. That route calls Centra's CheckoutAPI: `POST /api/checkout/products` with the product IDs
3. Returns product cards with: image, name, price (in NOK/SEK based on market), color swatches, "Legg i handlekurv" button
4. The "add to cart" action uses Centra's CheckoutAPI selection endpoint, adding to the existing Centra session/token

### 3.3 Deep Linking

Each product card links to the PDP at `/produkt/{centraDisplayUri}` (or whatever the existing Next.js PDP route is). The link includes a UTM parameter for attribution:

```
/produkt/lundhags-makke-pro-ms-pant?utm_source=shorts&utm_medium=video&utm_content={short.slug}
```

### 3.4 Centra Product Picker in Sanity

Build a custom Sanity input component that searches Centra's product catalog. This prevents editors from mistyping product IDs.

```
Custom input → Centra ShopAPI search → returns product list
Editor picks product → stores { centraProductId, centraDisplayUri, label }
```

This is a custom Sanity plugin, roughly 200 lines of React. There are community examples of similar "external resource" pickers in the Sanity ecosystem.

---

## 4. User-Generated Content (UGC)

### 4.1 Upload Flow

**Phase 1 (MVP):** Simple upload form on a dedicated page `/shorts/last-opp`.

```
User lands on upload page
  → Must be logged in (Centra customer auth)
  → Form: video file, title, caption, tag selection, product tagging (optional)
  → File validation: max 60s, max 200MB, 9:16 aspect ratio preferred, mp4/mov/webm
  → Upload goes to a Next.js API route → Mux direct upload (Mux provides a signed upload URL)
  → Simultaneously creates a Sanity document with source: 'ugc', moderationStatus: 'pending'
  → User sees "Takk! Vi gjennomgår videoen din." confirmation
```

**Phase 2:** In-app recording (mobile PWA). Use `MediaRecorder` API to capture directly from camera. This removes the friction of "upload a file" entirely.

### 4.2 Moderation Pipeline

Sanity Studio becomes the moderation dashboard:

1. **Custom desk structure** with a "UGC Moderation" section filtered to `moderationStatus == 'pending'`
2. Moderator sees: embedded Mux preview, creator info, any linked products
3. Actions: Approve (sets `moderationStatus: 'approved'`, `publishedAt: now()`), Reject (sets `moderationStatus: 'rejected'`, optionally sends email to creator), Request changes
4. **Sanity webhook** on status change triggers:
   - `approved` → sends a "Your video is live!" email via customer service tooling
   - `rejected` → sends rejection email with reason

### 4.3 Safety Rails

- **Mux moderation**: Mux has built-in content moderation that flags explicit content during transcoding. Wire this up via Mux webhooks to auto-reject flagged content.
- **File size/duration limits** enforced both client-side and server-side.
- **Rate limiting**: max 3 uploads per customer per 24h.

---

## 5. Next.js Implementation

### 5.1 Route Structure

```
/shorts                       → main feed (paginated, SSR first batch)
/shorts/[slug]                → deep link to specific short (SSR, OG meta for sharing)
/shorts/samling/[collection]  → collection feed
/shorts/tagg/[tag]            → tag feed
/shorts/last-opp              → UGC upload form (client-only, auth-gated)
/api/shorts/feed              → paginated feed API (cursor-based)
/api/shorts/products          → Centra product lookup for "Shop the look"
/api/shorts/upload            → Mux direct upload URL generator
```

### 5.2 Data Fetching Strategy

**Initial page load (SSR):**

```ts
// app/shorts/page.tsx — Server Component
const initialShorts = await sanityClient.fetch(`
  *[_type == "short" && moderationStatus == "approved"] | order(
    featured desc,
    sortOrder asc,
    publishedAt desc
  )[0...5] {
    _id,
    title,
    caption,
    slug,
    "playbackId": video.asset->playbackId,
    "thumbTime": video.asset->thumbTime,
    "duration": video.asset->data.duration,
    publishedAt,
    creator-> { name, handle, avatar },
    products[] {
      centraProductId,
      centraDisplayUri,
      label,
      timestampStart,
      timestampEnd,
    },
    tags[]-> { name, slug },
    department,
  }
`);

return <ShortsFeed initialShorts={initialShorts} />;
```

**Infinite scroll (client):**

```ts
// Cursor-based pagination via API route
// Client calls: /api/shorts/feed?cursor={lastPublishedAt}&limit=5
// API route fetches next 5 from Sanity, returns JSON
```

### 5.3 Video Preloading Strategy

This is where performance is won or lost. The prototype loads all videos eagerly which will not scale.

```
Slide N-1:  preload="none"     (already watched, release memory)
Slide N:    preload="auto"     (currently playing)
Slide N+1:  preload="metadata" (next up — load first frame + duration)
Slide N+2:  preload="none"     (not yet needed)
```

Use `@mux/mux-player-react` with `preload="metadata"` for next slide, and switch to `preload="auto"` when the slide enters the viewport via IntersectionObserver (same pattern as the prototype, but with smarter resource management).

**Key optimization:** Unmount `<MuxPlayer>` components that are more than 2 slides away from current. This prevents memory leaks on long scroll sessions. Use a virtualized list approach — only render 5 slides in the DOM at any time (current +/- 2).

### 5.4 Component Architecture

```
<ShortsPage>                          // Server Component
  <ShortsFeed initialShorts={data}>   // Client Component (scroll container)
    <ShortSlide>                      // per-video: player, overlays
      <MuxPlayer />
      <VideoOverlay>
        <ShortCaption />
        <VideoProgress />
      </VideoOverlay>
    </ShortSlide>
    <ShortsSidebar>                   // right side: thumbs, nav
      <Thumbnail />
    </ShortsSidebar>
    <ShortsInfoPanel>                 // left side: title, CTA
      <ShopTheLookButton />
    </ShortsInfoPanel>
    <ShopTheLookDrawer />             // slide-up product drawer
    <MuteToggle />
  </ShortsFeed>
</ShortsPage>
```

### 5.5 Sharing and SEO

For `/shorts/[slug]`, render full OG meta tags server-side:

```html
<meta property="og:type" content="video.other" />
<meta property="og:video" content="https://stream.mux.com/{playbackId}.m3u8" />
<meta property="og:video:width" content="1080" />
<meta property="og:video:height" content="1920" />
<meta property="og:image" content="https://image.mux.com/{playbackId}/thumbnail.jpg?time=2" />
```

This means shared links to Widforss Shorts render rich previews on Facebook, iMessage, Slack, etc. — driving traffic back to the owned platform instead of Instagram.

---

## 6. Features Beyond the Prototype

### 6.1 Timeline Product Tags

Products appear as floating pill overlays on the video at specific timestamps. The `timestampStart` and `timestampEnd` fields in the schema control when each tag shows.

```tsx
// During video playback, check current time against product timestamps
const visibleProducts = short.products.filter(p =>
  (!p.timestampStart || currentTime >= p.timestampStart) &&
  (!p.timestampEnd || currentTime <= p.timestampEnd)
);
// Render as small, tappable pills anchored to the video card
```

This turns every short into a shoppable video. When the creator holds up a jacket at second 12, the product tag appears and links directly to that jacket.

### 6.2 Engagement Metrics (via Mux Data)

Mux Data automatically tracks per-video:
- View count
- Average watch time and completion rate
- Rebuffer percentage
- Where viewers drop off (attention heatmap)

Expose a read-only "Analytics" tab in the Sanity Studio for each short using Mux's Data API:

```
GET https://api.mux.com/data/v1/metrics/views?filters[]=video_id:{assetId}
```

This tells the content team which shorts drive engagement and which fall flat — directly informing what kind of content to produce.

### 6.3 Hashtag / Collection Feeds

Tags and collections enable filtered feeds:
- `/shorts/tagg/vinterturer` — all shorts tagged "vinterturer"
- `/shorts/samling/vinterjakten-2026` — curated campaign collection

These also work as entry points from the main site. A PLP (product listing page) for winter jackets could have a "Se vinterturer" link that opens the shorts feed filtered to that tag.

### 6.4 Affiliate Tracking for UGC Creators

When a community creator's video drives a sale:

1. Creator has `affiliateCode` on their creator profile (e.g. `ERIK2026`)
2. When a viewer clicks "Shop the look" on that creator's short, the cart session gets a `utm_content=shorts-{slug}&affiliate={code}` parameter
3. Centra's order attribution captures this
4. Commission reporting via Centra's order export or a lightweight dashboard

This incentivizes creators to make content and share their shorts links. Essentially building a micro-influencer program within the platform.

### 6.5 AI Auto-Tagging (Phase 2)

When a video is uploaded to Mux, use a webhook to trigger a serverless function that:
1. Extracts a few keyframes from the Mux asset
2. Sends them to a vision model (Claude, GPT-4o) with the prompt: "What outdoor activities and gear categories are shown in this video?"
3. Auto-suggests tags and department in the Sanity document

This reduces editorial effort, especially for UGC where the uploader might not tag correctly.

### 6.6 Shorts Widget on PLPs and PDPs

A small horizontal carousel of shorts on product pages and category pages:

```
PDP for "Lundhags Makke Pro Pant"
  → GROQ query: *[_type == "short" && "{productId}" in products[].centraProductId]
  → Renders 3-5 thumbnail cards: "Se denna i aktion"
  → Click opens the full shorts feed, scrolled to that video
```

This creates a feedback loop: shorts drive product discovery, products drive shorts views.

### 6.7 "Save for Later" / Wishlist Integration

Let logged-in users heart/save shorts. Store as a lightweight array on their Centra customer profile or in a separate Sanity document:

```js
{ _type: 'savedShort', customer: 'centra-customer-id', short: { _ref: 'short-id' } }
```

### 6.8 Shorts in Email Campaigns

Mux provides animated GIF generation from any playback ID:

```
https://image.mux.com/{playbackId}/animated.gif?start=2&end=5&width=300
```

Use these in Klaviyo/email campaigns: "Se veckans populara shorts" with animated previews that link to the shorts feed. Much higher click-through than static images.

---

## 7. Mobile Experience

The prototype is desktop-first. Mobile is where this feature lives or dies.

### 7.1 Responsive Layout

On mobile (< 768px), the layout shifts dramatically:

```
Desktop:                          Mobile:
┌──────────────────────────┐     ┌────────────┐
│ [Info]  [Video]  [Thumbs]│     │            │
│  Left    Center   Right  │     │   Video    │
│                          │     │  (100vw    │
│                          │     │   100vh)   │
│                          │     │            │
│                          │     │ [overlays] │
└──────────────────────────┘     └────────────┘
```

- Video goes full viewport (100vw, 100dvh — use `dvh` for mobile browser chrome)
- Info panel overlays the bottom of the video (like Instagram Reels)
- Thumbnails sidebar disappears, replaced by swipe-only navigation
- "Shop the look" becomes a bottom sheet (like iOS share sheet)
- Progress bar stays at the bottom of the video card
- Mute button moves to top-right corner

### 7.2 Touch Gestures

```
Swipe up    → next short (snap scroll handles this natively)
Swipe down  → previous short
Tap         → pause/play toggle
Long press  → show product tags + share options
Swipe left  → open "Shop the look" drawer
```

The vertical snap scroll already works natively on mobile with CSS `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory`. The prototype's approach is correct — just needs the responsive layout shift.

### 7.3 Performance on Mobile

- **Preload only current + next video.** Mobile bandwidth and memory are constrained.
- **Use Mux's `max_resolution` parameter** to cap at 720p on mobile: `https://stream.mux.com/{playbackId}.m3u8?max_resolution=720p`
- **Intersection Observer with `rootMargin`** to start loading the next video 200px before it scrolls into view.
- **`loading="lazy"`** on all thumbnail images.
- **Service Worker caching** for the app shell (header, nav, CSS/JS) so repeat visits are instant.

### 7.4 PWA Considerations

Adding a basic PWA manifest makes sense:
- Allows "Add to Home Screen" on mobile — users who love the shorts feed can access it like an app
- Service worker caches the shell, fonts, and Widforss branding assets
- Does NOT need to work fully offline (video content requires network)
- Push notifications (Phase 3): "Nytt short fra @outdoorerik" — but only if the user has opted in

```json
// public/manifest.json
{
  "name": "Widforss Shorts",
  "short_name": "Shorts",
  "start_url": "/shorts",
  "display": "standalone",
  "background_color": "#1D1D1D",
  "theme_color": "#154A29",
  "icons": [...]
}
```

---

## 8. Architecture Summary

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Sanity Studio                     │
│  Content editing, UGC moderation, analytics view     │
│  Custom Centra product picker plugin                 │
└──────────┬────────────────────────┬──────────────────┘
           │ GROQ API              │ Mux plugin
           ▼                       ▼
┌──────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐
│   Sanity CDN     │    │       Mux           │
│  Content API     │    │ Transcode + Stream  │
│  (structured     │    │ HLS delivery        │
│   data)          │    │ Thumbnails          │
└────────┬─────────┘    │ Data (analytics)    │
         │              │ Content moderation  │
         │              └──────────┬──────────┘
         │                         │
         ▼                         ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Next.js Application                     │
│                                                      │
│  /shorts           → SSR feed page                   │
│  /shorts/[slug]    → SSR single short (OG meta)      │
│  /api/shorts/feed  → cursor-paginated feed           │
│  /api/shorts/products → Centra product lookup        │
│  /api/shorts/upload   → Mux direct upload URL        │
│  /api/webhooks/mux    → Mux event handler            │
│                                                      │
│  Client: MuxPlayer, snap scroll, shop drawer         │
└────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
              ┌─────────────────────┐
              │       Centra        │
              │  CheckoutAPI        │
              │  Product data       │
              │  Cart + checkout    │
              │  Customer auth      │
              │  Order attribution  │
              └─────────────────────┘
```

---

## 9. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Core Feed (2-3 weeks)
- Sanity schemas: `short`, `creator`, `shortsTag`, `shortsCollection`
- Mux integration in Sanity Studio
- Next.js `/shorts` page with SSR + infinite scroll
- Mux Player React with preloading strategy
- "Shop the look" drawer with Centra product lookup
- Responsive layout (desktop + mobile)
- Deep-linkable `/shorts/[slug]` with OG tags

### Phase 2: Polish and Discovery (1-2 weeks)
- Timeline product tags
- Tag and collection filtered feeds
- Shorts widget on PLPs/PDPs
- Centra product picker Sanity plugin
- Keyboard navigation (already prototyped)
- Share functionality (native share API on mobile, copy link on desktop)

### Phase 3: UGC Pipeline (2-3 weeks)
- Upload form with Mux direct uploads
- Sanity moderation dashboard (custom desk structure)
- Creator profiles
- Mux content moderation webhook
- Email notifications (approved/rejected)

### Phase 4: Growth Features (ongoing)
- Affiliate tracking for creators
- Engagement analytics in Studio
- AI auto-tagging
- PWA manifest + service worker
- Push notifications
- Animated GIF generation for email campaigns
- In-app recording (mobile)

---

## 10. Technical Decisions Summary

| Decision | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Video hosting | **Mux** | Sanity plugin, HLS, analytics, moderation, thumbnail API |
| Player | **@mux/mux-player-react** | Handles HLS, preload, analytics tracking |
| Product data at render time | **Centra CheckoutAPI** | Always-fresh price/stock, respects market (NO/SE) |
| Product data in Sanity | **ID + slug only** | Centra is source of truth, no sync needed |
| Feed pagination | **Cursor-based** (publishedAt) | Stable under new content, no offset drift |
| Mobile scroll | **CSS scroll-snap** | Native performance, no JS scroll libraries |
| UGC uploads | **Mux direct uploads** | No server bottleneck, handles large files |
| Moderation | **Sanity Studio** | Already the content team's tool, no new dashboard |
| Analytics | **Mux Data** | Free with Mux, per-video metrics |
| Sharing | **Native Web Share API** + fallback | Familiar UX on mobile |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

These are the files that would need to be created or modified in the existing Next.js + Sanity codebase:

- `sanity/schemas/documents/short.ts` — the core Short document schema (video, products, creator, tags, moderation)
- `app/shorts/page.tsx` — the SSR shorts feed page (initial data fetch, responsive layout, client hydration)
- `components/shorts/ShortsFeed.tsx` — the client-side feed component (snap scroll, video lifecycle, preloading, infinite pagination)
- `components/shorts/ShopTheLookDrawer.tsx` — the product drawer component (Centra API integration, add-to-cart, deep links to PDPs)
- `app/api/shorts/feed/route.ts` — the cursor-paginated feed API route (GROQ queries, pagination logic)

Context: Stefan is a product designer building a prototype for Widforss (Swedish/Norwegian outdoor e-commerce brand). Their production site runs Next.js + Sanity CMS + Centra (e-commerce platform). 

He's built a TikTok/Reels-style vertical video feed prototype (static HTML with 5 Instagram reel clips). The goal is to strengthen social proof and community engagement on their e-commerce site — Instagram reach is too low, so they want an owned solution.

The prototype currently has:
- Vertical video feed with auto-snap scrolling
- Thumbnails sidebar, progress bar, mute toggle
- Left panel with title, timestamp, "Shop the look" CTA
- Infinite scroll with reshuffled batches

I need a comprehensive plan for how this should work in production with their stack. Think about:

1. **Sanity schema design** — how to model Shorts content (video, metadata, linked products, creator info, tags)
2. **Video hosting/delivery** — where to store/serve vertical videos (Sanity assets? Mux? Cloudinary? CDN considerations for autoplay performance)
3. **Centra product linking** — how "Shop the look" connects to their product catalog, deep linking to PDPs
4. **User-generated content (UGC)** — how could customers/community upload their own clips? Upload flow, moderation pipeline, Sanity as moderation dashboard
5. **Next.js implementation** — SSR vs client, lazy loading, preloading strategy, mobile responsiveness
6. **Cool features they might not have thought of** — product tagging on video timeline, creator profiles, engagement metrics, hashtag/collection feeds, shoppable overlays, affiliate tracking for UGC creators, AI auto-tagging
7. **Mobile experience** — this is desktop-first but mobile is where short video lives. Touch gestures, fullscreen, PWA considerations

Be specific about Sanity schema shapes, API patterns, and architectural decisions. This is going to their IT department as a proposal alongside the prototype. Write it as a clear, structured plan they can evaluate.

Output as a well-structured markdown document. Be opinionated — recommend specific services where appropriate. Think Norwegian market (Vipps, Klarna already in use).

content.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the prototype. Let me produce the comprehensive production plan.

---

# Widforss Shorts: Production Implementation Plan

## 1. Sanity Schema Design

### 1.1 Core Document: `short`

```js
// schemas/documents/short.ts
{
  name: 'short',
  title: 'Short',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    // --- Video asset ---
    {
      name: 'video',
      title: 'Video',
      type: 'mux.video',           // via sanity-plugin-mux-input
      validation: Rule => Rule.required(),
    },
    {
      name: 'thumbnail',            // auto-generated from Mux, but overridable
      title: 'Custom Thumbnail',
      type: 'image',
      options: { hotspot: true },
    },

    // --- Metadata ---
    {
      name: 'title',
      type: 'string',
      validation: Rule => Rule.required().max(60),
    },
    {
      name: 'caption',
      type: 'text',
      rows: 2,
      validation: Rule => Rule.max(200),
    },
    {
      name: 'slug',
      type: 'slug',
      options: { source: 'title' },
    },

    // --- Linked products (Centra) ---
    {
      name: 'products',
      title: 'Shop the Look',
      type: 'array',
      of: [{
        type: 'object',
        name: 'taggedProduct',
        fields: [
          { name: 'centraProductId', type: 'string', title: 'Centra Product ID' },
          { name: 'centraDisplayUri', type: 'string', title: 'Centra Display URI (slug)' },
          { name: 'label', type: 'string', title: 'Display Label (e.g. "Lundhags Makke Pro Ms Pant")' },
          { name: 'timestampStart', type: 'number', title: 'Visible from (seconds)', description: 'When this product tag appears on timeline. Leave blank for always visible.' },
          { name: 'timestampEnd', type: 'number', title: 'Visible until (seconds)' },
        ],
        preview: {
          select: { title: 'label', subtitle: 'centraDisplayUri' },
        },
      }],
    },

    // --- Creator ---
    {
      name: 'creator',
      type: 'reference',
      to: [{ type: 'creator' }],
    },

    // --- Categorization ---
    {
      name: 'tags',
      type: 'array',
      of: [{ type: 'reference', to: [{ type: 'shortsTag' }] }],
    },
    {
      name: 'collection',
      title: 'Collection / Series',
      type: 'reference',
      to: [{ type: 'shortsCollection' }],
    },
    {
      name: 'department',
      title: 'Department',
      type: 'string',
      options: {
        list: ['herr', 'dame', 'barn', 'jakt', 'friluftsliv', 'hund'],
      },
    },

    // --- UGC / Moderation ---
    {
      name: 'source',
      type: 'string',
      options: { list: ['editorial', 'brand', 'ugc', 'influencer'] },
      initialValue: 'editorial',
    },
    {
      name: 'moderationStatus',
      type: 'string',
      options: { list: ['pending', 'approved', 'rejected'] },
      initialValue: 'approved',  // editorial content auto-approved
    },
    {
      name: 'moderationNotes',
      type: 'text',
      hidden: ({ document }) => document?.source === 'editorial',
    },

    // --- Publishing ---
    {
      name: 'publishedAt',
      type: 'datetime',
    },
    {
      name: 'featured',
      type: 'boolean',
      initialValue: false,
    },
    {
      name: 'sortOrder',
      type: 'number',
      description: 'Lower number = higher priority in feed. Leave blank for chronological.',
    },
  ],

  orderings: [
    { title: 'Newest first', name: 'publishedDesc', by: [{ field: 'publishedAt', direction: 'desc' }] },
    { title: 'Priority', name: 'sortOrder', by: [{ field: 'sortOrder', direction: 'asc' }] },
  ],

  preview: {
    select: {
      title: 'title',
      subtitle: 'caption',
      media: 'thumbnail',
    },
  },
}
```

### 1.2 Supporting Document: `creator`

```js
{
  name: 'creator',
  title: 'Creator',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'handle', type: 'slug', description: '@handle for display' },
    { name: 'avatar', type: 'image', options: { hotspot: true } },
    { name: 'instagramUrl', type: 'url' },
    { name: 'bio', type: 'text', rows: 2 },
    { name: 'type', type: 'string', options: { list: ['staff', 'ambassador', 'community'] } },
    { name: 'affiliateCode', type: 'string', description: 'Tracking code for commission attribution' },
  ],
}
```

### 1.3 Supporting Documents: `shortsTag` and `shortsCollection`

```js
// shortsTag — flat tag for hashtag-style filtering
{
  name: 'shortsTag',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },  // e.g. "vinterturer", "layering"
    { name: 'slug', type: 'slug', options: { source: 'name' } },
  ],
}

// shortsCollection — curated group, e.g. "Vinterjakten 2026"
{
  name: 'shortsCollection',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'title', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'slug', type: 'slug', options: { source: 'title' } },
    { name: 'description', type: 'text' },
    { name: 'coverImage', type: 'image' },
  ],
}
```

### 1.4 Singleton Document: `shortsFeedSettings`

```js
{
  name: 'shortsFeedSettings',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'pinnedShorts', type: 'array', of: [{ type: 'reference', to: [{ type: 'short' }] }], description: 'Always shown first in the feed, in this order' },
    { name: 'defaultDepartment', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'feedAlgorithm', type: 'string', options: { list: ['chronological', 'priority', 'engagement'] }, initialValue: 'priority' },
  ],
}
```

---

## 2. Video Hosting and Delivery

**Recommendation: Mux.**

Rationale:
- Mux has a first-party Sanity plugin (`sanity-plugin-mux-input`) making uploads trivial from the studio.
- Adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS) out of the box -- critical for vertical video where users are on 4G/5G.
- Automatic thumbnail generation at any timestamp (no manual screenshots).
- Mux Data gives you engagement analytics per-video (completion rate, rebuffer %, average watch time) with no extra integration.
- Storyboard sprites for seek preview come free.
- Mux is already used by other Scandinavian e-commerce companies on Sanity stacks.

**Why not Cloudinary or Sanity native assets:**
- Sanity's native file hosting has no transcoding, no adaptive bitrate, no analytics. Unusable for a video-first feature.
- Cloudinary's video is adequate but its Sanity integration is weaker and you do not get engagement analytics without extra tooling.

### Delivery Architecture

```
Sanity Studio (upload) → Mux (transcode + store)
                                    ↓
                        Mux Streaming URL (HLS)
                                    ↓
              Next.js <video> / hls.js / Mux Player React
```

**Mux Player React** (`@mux/mux-player-react`) is the recommended player component. It handles:
- HLS adaptive streaming
- Poster/thumbnail from playback ID
- Preload strategies (`metadata` vs `auto`)
- Mux Data tracking automatically

### Cost Estimate

Mux pricing (as of 2025): ~$0.007/min encoded + $0.003/min delivered. For a store with 100 shorts averaging 30 seconds each, initial encoding cost is about $3.50. Delivery at 50,000 views/month (25,000 minutes) would be roughly $75/month. Well within reason for an e-commerce site.

---

## 3. Centra Product Linking

### 3.1 Architecture

Centra is the source of truth for products, prices, and stock. Sanity stores only the reference (product ID + display URI), never the product data itself.

```
Sanity (short.products[].centraProductId)
            ↓ at render time
Next.js API route or server component
            ↓
Centra CheckoutAPI / ShopAPI
            ↓
Live price, stock, images, variant data
```

### 3.2 "Shop the Look" Drawer

When the user clicks "Shop the look", a slide-up drawer appears over the video (not a page navigation). This drawer:

1. Calls a Next.js API route: `GET /api/shorts/products?ids=123,456,789`
2. That route calls Centra's CheckoutAPI: `POST /api/checkout/products` with the product IDs
3. Returns product cards with: image, name, price (in NOK/SEK based on market), color swatches, "Legg i handlekurv" button
4. The "add to cart" action uses Centra's CheckoutAPI selection endpoint, adding to the existing Centra session/token

### 3.3 Deep Linking

Each product card links to the PDP at `/produkt/{centraDisplayUri}` (or whatever the existing Next.js PDP route is). The link includes a UTM parameter for attribution:

```
/produkt/lundhags-makke-pro-ms-pant?utm_source=shorts&utm_medium=video&utm_content={short.slug}
```

### 3.4 Centra Product Picker in Sanity

Build a custom Sanity input component that searches Centra's product catalog. This prevents editors from mistyping product IDs.

```
Custom input → Centra ShopAPI search → returns product list
Editor picks product → stores { centraProductId, centraDisplayUri, label }
```

This is a custom Sanity plugin, roughly 200 lines of React. There are community examples of similar "external resource" pickers in the Sanity ecosystem.

---

## 4. User-Generated Content (UGC)

### 4.1 Upload Flow

**Phase 1 (MVP):** Simple upload form on a dedicated page `/shorts/last-opp`.

```
User lands on upload page
  → Must be logged in (Centra customer auth)
  → Form: video file, title, caption, tag selection, product tagging (optional)
  → File validation: max 60s, max 200MB, 9:16 aspect ratio preferred, mp4/mov/webm
  → Upload goes to a Next.js API route → Mux direct upload (Mux provides a signed upload URL)
  → Simultaneously creates a Sanity document with source: 'ugc', moderationStatus: 'pending'
  → User sees "Takk! Vi gjennomgår videoen din." confirmation
```

**Phase 2:** In-app recording (mobile PWA). Use `MediaRecorder` API to capture directly from camera. This removes the friction of "upload a file" entirely.

### 4.2 Moderation Pipeline

Sanity Studio becomes the moderation dashboard:

1. **Custom desk structure** with a "UGC Moderation" section filtered to `moderationStatus == 'pending'`
2. Moderator sees: embedded Mux preview, creator info, any linked products
3. Actions: Approve (sets `moderationStatus: 'approved'`, `publishedAt: now()`), Reject (sets `moderationStatus: 'rejected'`, optionally sends email to creator), Request changes
4. **Sanity webhook** on status change triggers:
   - `approved` → sends a "Your video is live!" email via customer service tooling
   - `rejected` → sends rejection email with reason

### 4.3 Safety Rails

- **Mux moderation**: Mux has built-in content moderation that flags explicit content during transcoding. Wire this up via Mux webhooks to auto-reject flagged content.
- **File size/duration limits** enforced both client-side and server-side.
- **Rate limiting**: max 3 uploads per customer per 24h.

---

## 5. Next.js Implementation

### 5.1 Route Structure

```
/shorts                       → main feed (paginated, SSR first batch)
/shorts/[slug]                → deep link to specific short (SSR, OG meta for sharing)
/shorts/samling/[collection]  → collection feed
/shorts/tagg/[tag]            → tag feed
/shorts/last-opp              → UGC upload form (client-only, auth-gated)
/api/shorts/feed              → paginated feed API (cursor-based)
/api/shorts/products          → Centra product lookup for "Shop the look"
/api/shorts/upload            → Mux direct upload URL generator
```

### 5.2 Data Fetching Strategy

**Initial page load (SSR):**

```ts
// app/shorts/page.tsx — Server Component
const initialShorts = await sanityClient.fetch(`
  *[_type == "short" && moderationStatus == "approved"] | order(
    featured desc,
    sortOrder asc,
    publishedAt desc
  )[0...5] {
    _id,
    title,
    caption,
    slug,
    "playbackId": video.asset->playbackId,
    "thumbTime": video.asset->thumbTime,
    "duration": video.asset->data.duration,
    publishedAt,
    creator-> { name, handle, avatar },
    products[] {
      centraProductId,
      centraDisplayUri,
      label,
      timestampStart,
      timestampEnd,
    },
    tags[]-> { name, slug },
    department,
  }
`);

return <ShortsFeed initialShorts={initialShorts} />;
```

**Infinite scroll (client):**

```ts
// Cursor-based pagination via API route
// Client calls: /api/shorts/feed?cursor={lastPublishedAt}&limit=5
// API route fetches next 5 from Sanity, returns JSON
```

### 5.3 Video Preloading Strategy

This is where performance is won or lost. The prototype loads all videos eagerly which will not scale.

```
Slide N-1:  preload="none"     (already watched, release memory)
Slide N:    preload="auto"     (currently playing)
Slide N+1:  preload="metadata" (next up — load first frame + duration)
Slide N+2:  preload="none"     (not yet needed)
```

Use `@mux/mux-player-react` with `preload="metadata"` for next slide, and switch to `preload="auto"` when the slide enters the viewport via IntersectionObserver (same pattern as the prototype, but with smarter resource management).

**Key optimization:** Unmount `<MuxPlayer>` components that are more than 2 slides away from current. This prevents memory leaks on long scroll sessions. Use a virtualized list approach — only render 5 slides in the DOM at any time (current +/- 2).

### 5.4 Component Architecture

```
<ShortsPage>                          // Server Component
  <ShortsFeed initialShorts={data}>   // Client Component (scroll container)
    <ShortSlide>                      // per-video: player, overlays
      <MuxPlayer />
      <VideoOverlay>
        <ShortCaption />
        <VideoProgress />
      </VideoOverlay>
    </ShortSlide>
    <ShortsSidebar>                   // right side: thumbs, nav
      <Thumbnail />
    </ShortsSidebar>
    <ShortsInfoPanel>                 // left side: title, CTA
      <ShopTheLookButton />
    </ShortsInfoPanel>
    <ShopTheLookDrawer />             // slide-up product drawer
    <MuteToggle />
  </ShortsFeed>
</ShortsPage>
```

### 5.5 Sharing and SEO

For `/shorts/[slug]`, render full OG meta tags server-side:

```html
<meta property="og:type" content="video.other" />
<meta property="og:video" content="https://stream.mux.com/{playbackId}.m3u8" />
<meta property="og:video:width" content="1080" />
<meta property="og:video:height" content="1920" />
<meta property="og:image" content="https://image.mux.com/{playbackId}/thumbnail.jpg?time=2" />
```

This means shared links to Widforss Shorts render rich previews on Facebook, iMessage, Slack, etc. — driving traffic back to the owned platform instead of Instagram.

---

## 6. Features Beyond the Prototype

### 6.1 Timeline Product Tags

Products appear as floating pill overlays on the video at specific timestamps. The `timestampStart` and `timestampEnd` fields in the schema control when each tag shows.

```tsx
// During video playback, check current time against product timestamps
const visibleProducts = short.products.filter(p =>
  (!p.timestampStart || currentTime >= p.timestampStart) &&
  (!p.timestampEnd || currentTime <= p.timestampEnd)
);
// Render as small, tappable pills anchored to the video card
```

This turns every short into a shoppable video. When the creator holds up a jacket at second 12, the product tag appears and links directly to that jacket.

### 6.2 Engagement Metrics (via Mux Data)

Mux Data automatically tracks per-video:
- View count
- Average watch time and completion rate
- Rebuffer percentage
- Where viewers drop off (attention heatmap)

Expose a read-only "Analytics" tab in the Sanity Studio for each short using Mux's Data API:

```
GET https://api.mux.com/data/v1/metrics/views?filters[]=video_id:{assetId}
```

This tells the content team which shorts drive engagement and which fall flat — directly informing what kind of content to produce.

### 6.3 Hashtag / Collection Feeds

Tags and collections enable filtered feeds:
- `/shorts/tagg/vinterturer` — all shorts tagged "vinterturer"
- `/shorts/samling/vinterjakten-2026` — curated campaign collection

These also work as entry points from the main site. A PLP (product listing page) for winter jackets could have a "Se vinterturer" link that opens the shorts feed filtered to that tag.

### 6.4 Affiliate Tracking for UGC Creators

When a community creator's video drives a sale:

1. Creator has `affiliateCode` on their creator profile (e.g. `ERIK2026`)
2. When a viewer clicks "Shop the look" on that creator's short, the cart session gets a `utm_content=shorts-{slug}&affiliate={code}` parameter
3. Centra's order attribution captures this
4. Commission reporting via Centra's order export or a lightweight dashboard

This incentivizes creators to make content and share their shorts links. Essentially building a micro-influencer program within the platform.

### 6.5 AI Auto-Tagging (Phase 2)

When a video is uploaded to Mux, use a webhook to trigger a serverless function that:
1. Extracts a few keyframes from the Mux asset
2. Sends them to a vision model (Claude, GPT-4o) with the prompt: "What outdoor activities and gear categories are shown in this video?"
3. Auto-suggests tags and department in the Sanity document

This reduces editorial effort, especially for UGC where the uploader might not tag correctly.

### 6.6 Shorts Widget on PLPs and PDPs

A small horizontal carousel of shorts on product pages and category pages:

```
PDP for "Lundhags Makke Pro Pant"
  → GROQ query: *[_type == "short" && "{productId}" in products[].centraProductId]
  → Renders 3-5 thumbnail cards: "Se denna i aktion"
  → Click opens the full shorts feed, scrolled to that video
```

This creates a feedback loop: shorts drive product discovery, products drive shorts views.

### 6.7 "Save for Later" / Wishlist Integration

Let logged-in users heart/save shorts. Store as a lightweight array on their Centra customer profile or in a separate Sanity document:

```js
{ _type: 'savedShort', customer: 'centra-customer-id', short: { _ref: 'short-id' } }
```

### 6.8 Shorts in Email Campaigns

Mux provides animated GIF generation from any playback ID:

```
https://image.mux.com/{playbackId}/animated.gif?start=2&end=5&width=300
```

Use these in Klaviyo/email campaigns: "Se veckans populara shorts" with animated previews that link to the shorts feed. Much higher click-through than static images.

---

## 7. Mobile Experience

The prototype is desktop-first. Mobile is where this feature lives or dies.

### 7.1 Responsive Layout

On mobile (< 768px), the layout shifts dramatically:

```
Desktop:                          Mobile:
┌──────────────────────────┐     ┌────────────┐
│ [Info]  [Video]  [Thumbs]│     │            │
│  Left    Center   Right  │     │   Video    │
│                          │     │  (100vw    │
│                          │     │   100vh)   │
│                          │     │            │
│                          │     │ [overlays] │
└──────────────────────────┘     └────────────┘
```

- Video goes full viewport (100vw, 100dvh — use `dvh` for mobile browser chrome)
- Info panel overlays the bottom of the video (like Instagram Reels)
- Thumbnails sidebar disappears, replaced by swipe-only navigation
- "Shop the look" becomes a bottom sheet (like iOS share sheet)
- Progress bar stays at the bottom of the video card
- Mute button moves to top-right corner

### 7.2 Touch Gestures

```
Swipe up    → next short (snap scroll handles this natively)
Swipe down  → previous short
Tap         → pause/play toggle
Long press  → show product tags + share options
Swipe left  → open "Shop the look" drawer
```

The vertical snap scroll already works natively on mobile with CSS `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory`. The prototype's approach is correct — just needs the responsive layout shift.

### 7.3 Performance on Mobile

- **Preload only current + next video.** Mobile bandwidth and memory are constrained.
- **Use Mux's `max_resolution` parameter** to cap at 720p on mobile: `https://stream.mux.com/{playbackId}.m3u8?max_resolution=720p`
- **Intersection Observer with `rootMargin`** to start loading the next video 200px before it scrolls into view.
- **`loading="lazy"`** on all thumbnail images.
- **Service Worker caching** for the app shell (header, nav, CSS/JS) so repeat visits are instant.

### 7.4 PWA Considerations

Adding a basic PWA manifest makes sense:
- Allows "Add to Home Screen" on mobile — users who love the shorts feed can access it like an app
- Service worker caches the shell, fonts, and Widforss branding assets
- Does NOT need to work fully offline (video content requires network)
- Push notifications (Phase 3): "Nytt short fra @outdoorerik" — but only if the user has opted in

```json
// public/manifest.json
{
  "name": "Widforss Shorts",
  "short_name": "Shorts",
  "start_url": "/shorts",
  "display": "standalone",
  "background_color": "#1D1D1D",
  "theme_color": "#154A29",
  "icons": [...]
}
```

---

## 8. Architecture Summary

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Sanity Studio                     │
│  Content editing, UGC moderation, analytics view     │
│  Custom Centra product picker plugin                 │
└──────────┬────────────────────────┬──────────────────┘
           │ GROQ API              │ Mux plugin
           ▼                       ▼
┌──────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐
│   Sanity CDN     │    │       Mux           │
│  Content API     │    │ Transcode + Stream  │
│  (structured     │    │ HLS delivery        │
│   data)          │    │ Thumbnails          │
└────────┬─────────┘    │ Data (analytics)    │
         │              │ Content moderation  │
         │              └──────────┬──────────┘
         │                         │
         ▼                         ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Next.js Application                     │
│                                                      │
│  /shorts           → SSR feed page                   │
│  /shorts/[slug]    → SSR single short (OG meta)      │
│  /api/shorts/feed  → cursor-paginated feed           │
│  /api/shorts/products → Centra product lookup        │
│  /api/shorts/upload   → Mux direct upload URL        │
│  /api/webhooks/mux    → Mux event handler            │
│                                                      │
│  Client: MuxPlayer, snap scroll, shop drawer         │
└────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
              ┌─────────────────────┐
              │       Centra        │
              │  CheckoutAPI        │
              │  Product data       │
              │  Cart + checkout    │
              │  Customer auth      │
              │  Order attribution  │
              └─────────────────────┘
```

---

## 9. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Core Feed (2-3 weeks)
- Sanity schemas: `short`, `creator`, `shortsTag`, `shortsCollection`
- Mux integration in Sanity Studio
- Next.js `/shorts` page with SSR + infinite scroll
- Mux Player React with preloading strategy
- "Shop the look" drawer with Centra product lookup
- Responsive layout (desktop + mobile)
- Deep-linkable `/shorts/[slug]` with OG tags

### Phase 2: Polish and Discovery (1-2 weeks)
- Timeline product tags
- Tag and collection filtered feeds
- Shorts widget on PLPs/PDPs
- Centra product picker Sanity plugin
- Keyboard navigation (already prototyped)
- Share functionality (native share API on mobile, copy link on desktop)

### Phase 3: UGC Pipeline (2-3 weeks)
- Upload form with Mux direct uploads
- Sanity moderation dashboard (custom desk structure)
- Creator profiles
- Mux content moderation webhook
- Email notifications (approved/rejected)

### Phase 4: Growth Features (ongoing)
- Affiliate tracking for creators
- Engagement analytics in Studio
- AI auto-tagging
- PWA manifest + service worker
- Push notifications
- Animated GIF generation for email campaigns
- In-app recording (mobile)

---

## 10. Technical Decisions Summary

| Decision | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Video hosting | **Mux** | Sanity plugin, HLS, analytics, moderation, thumbnail API |
| Player | **@mux/mux-player-react** | Handles HLS, preload, analytics tracking |
| Product data at render time | **Centra CheckoutAPI** | Always-fresh price/stock, respects market (NO/SE) |
| Product data in Sanity | **ID + slug only** | Centra is source of truth, no sync needed |
| Feed pagination | **Cursor-based** (publishedAt) | Stable under new content, no offset drift |
| Mobile scroll | **CSS scroll-snap** | Native performance, no JS scroll libraries |
| UGC uploads | **Mux direct uploads** | No server bottleneck, handles large files |
| Moderation | **Sanity Studio** | Already the content team's tool, no new dashboard |
| Analytics | **Mux Data** | Free with Mux, per-video metrics |
| Sharing | **Native Web Share API** + fallback | Familiar UX on mobile |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

# Widforss Shorts — Production Implementation Plan

## 1. Sanity Schema Design

### Core Document: `short`

```js
{
  name: 'short',
  title: 'Short',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    // Video asset (Mux)
    { name: 'video', type: 'mux.video', validation: Rule => Rule.required() },
    { name: 'thumbnail', type: 'image', options: { hotspot: true } },

    // Metadata
    { name: 'title', type: 'string', validation: Rule => Rule.required().max(60) },
    { name: 'caption', type: 'text', rows: 2, validation: Rule => Rule.max(200) },
    { name: 'slug', type: 'slug', options: { source: 'title' } },

    // Linked products (Centra)
    {
      name: 'products',
      title: 'Shop the Look',
      type: 'array',
      of: [{
        type: 'object',
        name: 'taggedProduct',
        fields: [
          { name: 'centraProductId', type: 'string' },
          { name: 'centraDisplayUri', type: 'string' },
          { name: 'label', type: 'string' },
          { name: 'timestampStart', type: 'number', description: 'Show from (seconds)' },
          { name: 'timestampEnd', type: 'number', description: 'Show until (seconds)' },
        ],
      }],
    },

    // Creator
    { name: 'creator', type: 'reference', to: [{ type: 'creator' }] },

    // Categorization
    { name: 'tags', type: 'array', of: [{ type: 'reference', to: [{ type: 'shortsTag' }] }] },
    { name: 'collection', type: 'reference', to: [{ type: 'shortsCollection' }] },
    {
      name: 'department',
      type: 'string',
      options: { list: ['herr', 'dame', 'barn', 'jakt', 'friluftsliv', 'hund'] },
    },

    // UGC / Moderation
    {
      name: 'source',
      type: 'string',
      options: { list: ['editorial', 'brand', 'ugc', 'influencer'] },
      initialValue: 'editorial',
    },
    {
      name: 'moderationStatus',
      type: 'string',
      options: { list: ['pending', 'approved', 'rejected'] },
      initialValue: 'approved',
    },
    { name: 'moderationNotes', type: 'text' },

    // Publishing
    { name: 'publishedAt', type: 'datetime' },
    { name: 'featured', type: 'boolean', initialValue: false },
    { name: 'sortOrder', type: 'number' },
  ],
}
```

### Supporting: `creator`

```js
{
  name: 'creator',
  type: 'document',
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'handle', type: 'slug' },
    { name: 'avatar', type: 'image', options: { hotspot: true } },
    { name: 'instagramUrl', type: 'url' },
    { name: 'bio', type: 'text', rows: 2 },
    { name: 'type', type: 'string', options: { list: ['staff', 'ambassador', 'community'] } },
    { name: 'affiliateCode', type: 'string' },
  ],
}
```

### Supporting: `shortsTag` + `shortsCollection`

Tags for hashtag-style filtering, collections for curated campaigns (e.g. "Vinterjakten 2026").

---

## 2. Video Hosting — Mux

**Why Mux:**
- First-party Sanity plugin (`sanity-plugin-mux-input`) — upload directly from Studio
- Adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS) out of the box
- Auto-generated thumbnails at any timestamp
- Mux Data gives engagement analytics per video (completion rate, drop-off points, rebuffer %)
- Content moderation built in
- Animated GIF generation for email campaigns

**Cost estimate:** ~100 shorts at 30s each, 50k views/month = ~$75/month.

**Delivery chain:**
```
Sanity Studio → Mux (transcode) → HLS stream → @mux/mux-player-react
```

---

## 3. Centra Product Linking

Sanity stores only `centraProductId` + `centraDisplayUri` — never product data.

**"Shop the Look" flow:**
1. User clicks CTA → slide-up drawer appears over video
2. Next.js API route calls Centra CheckoutAPI with product IDs
3. Returns live price, stock, images, variants (respects NO/SE market)
4. "Legg i handlekurv" adds to existing Centra cart session

**Deep links:** `/produkt/{slug}?utm_source=shorts&utm_content={short.slug}`

**Sanity product picker:** Custom input component that searches Centra's product catalog, so editors never mistype IDs.

---

## 4. User-Generated Content (UGC)

### Upload Flow (Phase 1)
- Dedicated page: `/shorts/last-opp`
- Requires Centra customer login
- Form: video, title, caption, tags, optional product tagging
- Validation: max 60s, max 200MB, 9:16 preferred
- Upload via Mux direct upload (signed URL, no server bottleneck)
- Creates Sanity doc with `source: 'ugc'`, `moderationStatus: 'pending'`

### Moderation
- Custom Sanity Studio desk structure: "UGC Moderation" filtered to pending
- Actions: Approve / Reject / Request changes
- Mux auto-flags explicit content via webhook
- Email notifications on status change

### Phase 2: In-app recording
- Mobile PWA with `MediaRecorder` API — capture from camera directly
- Removes "upload a file" friction entirely

---

## 5. Next.js Implementation

### Route Structure
```
/shorts                     → SSR feed (first 5 videos)
/shorts/[slug]              → deep link to single short (OG meta for sharing)
/shorts/samling/[collection]→ collection feed
/shorts/tagg/[tag]          → tag feed
/shorts/last-opp            → UGC upload (auth-gated)
/api/shorts/feed            → cursor-paginated feed
/api/shorts/products        → Centra product lookup
/api/shorts/upload          → Mux upload URL generator
```

### Video Preloading Strategy
```
Slide N-1:  preload="none"     (watched, release memory)
Slide N:    preload="auto"     (playing)
Slide N+1:  preload="metadata" (next up)
Slide N+2+: preload="none"     (not yet needed)
```

Unmount `<MuxPlayer>` components more than 2 slides from current to prevent memory leaks on infinite scroll.

### Sharing / SEO
Server-render OG tags on `/shorts/[slug]`:
```html
<meta property="og:type" content="video.other" />
<meta property="og:video" content="https://stream.mux.com/{id}.m3u8" />
<meta property="og:image" content="https://image.mux.com/{id}/thumbnail.jpg" />
```
Shared links render rich previews on social platforms — driving traffic back to Widforss instead of Instagram.

---

## 6. Features Beyond the Prototype

### Timeline Product Tags
Products appear as floating pills on the video at specific timestamps. When creator holds up a jacket at second 12, the tag appears and links to that jacket.

### Shorts Widget on PLPs/PDPs
Small carousel on product pages: "Se denna i aktion" — query all shorts that reference that product. Creates a feedback loop between shorts and product discovery.

### Engagement Analytics
Mux Data per-video: views, completion rate, drop-off heatmap. Expose as read-only tab in Sanity Studio so content team knows what works.

### Affiliate Tracking for Creators
Creator `affiliateCode` is attached to cart sessions when viewers buy through their shorts. Enables commission reporting and incentivizes UGC.

### AI Auto-Tagging
On video upload → extract keyframes → vision model suggests tags and department. Reduces editorial effort, especially for UGC.

### Animated GIFs for Email
Mux generates animated GIFs from any playback ID:
```
https://image.mux.com/{id}/animated.gif?start=2&end=5&width=300
```
Use in Klaviyo campaigns: "Se veckans populara shorts" — much higher click-through than static images.

---

## 7. Mobile Experience

### Layout Shift (< 768px)
- Video goes full viewport (100vw, 100dvh)
- Info overlays bottom of video (like Reels)
- Thumbnails sidebar disappears — swipe only
- "Shop the look" becomes bottom sheet
- Mute button top-right

### Touch Gestures
- Swipe up/down: next/prev (CSS scroll-snap handles natively)
- Tap: pause/play
- Long press: product tags + share
- Swipe left: open shop drawer

### Performance
- Cap at 720p on mobile: `stream.mux.com/{id}.m3u8?max_resolution=720p`
- IntersectionObserver with rootMargin to preload next video 200px early
- Service worker caches shell, fonts, branding

### PWA
Basic manifest enables "Add to Home Screen" — repeat visitors get app-like access. Push notifications in Phase 3.

---

## 8. Architecture

```
Sanity Studio (content + moderation)
        │                    │
    GROQ API            Mux plugin
        ▼                    ▼
   Sanity CDN              Mux
   (metadata)         (video stream)
        │                    │
        └────────┬───────────┘
                 ▼
          Next.js App
         /shorts pages
         /api routes
                 │
                 ▼
              Centra
        (products, cart,
         customer auth)
```

---

## 9. Phases

| Phase | Scope | Est. |
|-------|-------|------|
| **1: Core Feed** | Sanity schemas, Mux, Next.js /shorts, Shop the look drawer, responsive | 2-3 weeks |
| **2: Discovery** | Timeline product tags, tag/collection feeds, shorts on PLPs/PDPs, share | 1-2 weeks |
| **3: UGC** | Upload form, Mux direct upload, moderation dashboard, creator profiles | 2-3 weeks |
| **4: Growth** | Affiliate tracking, analytics in Studio, AI tagging, PWA, email GIFs | Ongoing |

---

## 10. Key Decisions

| Decision | Recommendation | Why |
|----------|---------------|-----|
| Video hosting | Mux | Sanity plugin, HLS, analytics, moderation |
| Player | @mux/mux-player-react | Handles HLS, preload, analytics |
| Product data source | Centra CheckoutAPI at render time | Fresh price/stock, market-aware |
| Product data in Sanity | ID + slug only | No sync needed, Centra is truth |
| Feed pagination | Cursor-based (publishedAt) | Stable under new content |
| Mobile scroll | CSS scroll-snap | Native performance, no JS library |
| UGC uploads | Mux direct uploads | No server bottleneck |
| Moderation | Sanity Studio | Content team's existing tool |

message.content: interssant hvordan ville en bruker lastet opp video på widforss da?

content.text:
Tenker meg noe slikt:

**Enkleste veien inn:** En "Del din opplevelse"-knapp synlig i shorts-feeden (f.eks. under thumbnails på sidebar, eller som en fast CTA i headeren på /shorts-siden). På mobil kunne den ligge som en floating action button.

**Flyten:**

1. **Bruker trykker "Del din opplevelse"**
2. **Innlogging** — må ha Centra-konto. Har de ikke det, oppretter de en (samme flow som checkout-registrering). Lav terskel — e-post + passord, eller "Logg inn med Vipps" hvis det er integrert.
3. **Upload-skjerm** (`/shorts/last-opp`):
   - Velg video fra kamerarull (mobil) eller filutforsker (desktop)
   - Live preview av videoen i 9:16-format med crop-verktøy hvis feil ratio
   - Validering: maks 60 sek, maks 200 MB, viser feilmelding umiddelbart hvis for lang/stor
   - Felt: tittel (valgfritt), kort beskrivelse, velg tags fra forhåndsdefinert liste ("Vinterturer", "Jakt", "Layering" osv.)
   - **Produkttagging** (valgfritt): "Bruker du Widforss-produkter i videoen?" → søkefelt som slår opp i Centra-katalogen → bruker velger produkt(er)
4. **Upload** — filen går direkte til Mux via signed URL (ikke via deres server, så den håndterer store filer uten timeout). Progressbar vises.
5. **Bekreftelse** — "Takk! Vi ser over videoen din og publiserer den innen 24 timer." E-post-notifikasjon når den er godkjent.

**Fase 2 (mobil PWA):** Dropp "velg fil" — bruk `MediaRecorder` API så brukeren filmer direkte i appen. Åpne kamera → film → trim → tag → send. Fjerner all friksjon.

**Moderering bak kulissene:**
- Videoen lander i Sanity Studio som et dokument med `status: pending`
- Redaksjonen har en "UGC-kø" i Studio — preview, godkjenn/avvis med ett klikk
- Mux flagger automatisk upassende innhold under transkoding
- Avvist → e-post med begrunnelse

**Incentiv for å laste opp:**
- Kreatøren får en profilside (`/shorts/creator/@handle`) med alle sine shorts
- Hvis deres video driver salg via "Shop the look" → provisjon via affiliate-kode
- Potensielt: "Månedens bidrag" som pinnes øverst i feeden

Vil du at jeg bygger en mockup av upload-flyten inn i prototypen?

content.text:
Ja, absolutt. Tenker tre nivåer:

**1. "Shop the look" — allerede i prototypen**
Den grønne pillen til venstre. I produksjon åpner den en drawer/bottom sheet med alle produktene tagget til den videoen — med live bilde, pris og "Legg i handlekurv" direkte fra Centra.

**2. Produkt-pills på tidslinje**
Når kreatøren holder opp en jakke på sekund 8, popper det opp en liten pill over videoen: `[Lundhags Makke Pro — 2 499 kr]`. Forsvinner igjen på sekund 14. Styrt av `timestampStart`/`timestampEnd` i Sanity-skjemaet. Samme interaksjon som shoppable video på YouTube/Instagram.

**3. Produktthumbnails i sidebar**
Istedenfor (eller i tillegg til) video-thumbnails på høyre side — vis produktbilder fra Centra for det som tagges i den aktive videoen. Små produktkort med bilde + pris. Klikk → PDP eller add to cart.

**Bonus-tilkobling tilbake:**
- På PDP for "Lundhags Makke Pro" viser du en liten shorts-karusell: "Se denne i bruk" — alle shorts som har tagget det produktet
- På kategori/PLP: "Populart i community" — shorts filtrert til den kategorien
- I checkout: "Andre som kjopte dette filmet seg selv" — social proof loop

Hele greia skaper en sirkel: shorts → produktsalg → flere shorts.
